description: Relying on the app continuing to run in the background, so the system suspends or terminates it and in-flight work is lost.
body: Failure mode: the app starts long-running work (upload, processing) expecting to keep executing after the user backgrounds it, but iOS suspends the process and may terminate it, dropping the work with no callback. Detection signals: uploads/tasks that fail to complete when the user leaves the app; work that only finishes if the app stays foregrounded; lost progress after the system reclaims the app. Do not assume indefinite background execution. Safer fallback: use the appropriate background API (BGTaskScheduler, begin/endBackgroundTask, or a specific background mode), persist progress so it can resume, and handle suspension and relaunch explicitly.
